Name: Strawberry
Sun: Full sun. Aim for at least 6 hours direct sun; 8-10 hours is ideal for stronger fruiting.
Water: Keep evenly moist. About 1 inch / 25mm water per week; containers may need more frequent checks. Water early and avoid wetting crown, flowers and fruit.
Fertiliser: Containers: feed fortnightly in the growing season. Start with general liquid feed, then switch to high-potassium feed when flower buds form.
Pruning: Remove unwanted runners. After harvest, remove old leaves; for repeat-fruiting types remove dead, damaged or diseased leaves during clean-up.
Flowering: Self-fertile but bees help pollination. Protect flowers from late frost; fruiting time depends on variety, often early summer to autumn.
Notes: Use fertile, well-drained, slightly acidic mix. Mulch with straw when fruit develops to keep berries clean and reduce rot.
